nodes.label: No sanctioned local IPC path (System.Net blocked)
nodes.rationale: System.Net is blocked in the sandbox — HttpListener, WebSocket, TcpListener are unavailable. File-based polling in a shared temp directory is the only workaround, which is slow and unreliable. A whitelisted localhost socket or named pipe is requested.
nodes.norm_label: no sanctioned local ipc path (system.net blocked)

nodes.label: No vpcf particle compiler access from addon
nodes.rationale: AssetSystem.RegisterFile(path).Compile() works for .vmat but fails with 'Failed to find compiler for .vpcf'. The particle compiler is not registered in the editor assembly that addons run in.
nodes.norm_label: no vpcf particle compiler access from addon

nodes.label: System.Math/MathF unavailable in sandbox, MathX incomplete
nodes.rationale: Sandboxed code cannot call MathF.Sin, Math.Abs, etc. MathX lacks Abs, Min, Max, Sin, Cos, Tan, Atan2, Sqrt, Pow, PI/Tau. Requesting System.Math/MathF whitelisted or MathX expanded.
nodes.norm_label: system.math/mathf unavailable in sandbox, mathx incomplete

nodes.label: Cloud.Model only accepts string literals
nodes.rationale: Cloud.Model, Cloud.Texture, Cloud.Sound are source-generated and accept only string literals, not runtime variables. A runtime equivalent like Cloud.Load(string ident) is requested.

nodes.label: SetFaceMaterial and Color tint broken on PolygonMesh
nodes.rationale: MeshComponent.SetFaceMaterial and MeshComponent.Color have no visible effect on a code-built PolygonMesh — the mesh keeps showing its default material despite calls succeeding. Suspected rendering bug.
nodes.norm_label: setfacematerial and color tint broken on polygonmesh

nodes.label: No API to capture editor viewport to image
nodes.rationale: Screenshots can only be rendered from the scene's Main Camera. An API to render the active viewport or any given camera/transform to an image would make visual checks easier.
nodes.norm_label: no api to capture editor viewport to image

nodes.label: Batch 17 Visual & Atmosphere Bridge Tools
nodes.rationale: add_light, set_fog, add_post_process, set_skybox, add_envmap_probe, apply_atmosphere, apply_post_fx_look — 7 tools making up Batch 17. Each maps to a C# IBridgeHandler and a TS Zod schema in visuals.ts.

nodes.label: Screenshot loop validation methodology for bridge tools
nodes.rationale: Bridge addon is editor C# and cannot be unit-tested outside s&box. The test harness for each tool is: build → sync → hotload → invoke → take_screenshot → read PNG → confirm visual result.
nodes.norm_label: screenshot loop validation methodology for bridge tools

nodes.label: Verified Light component API (DirectionalLight, PointLight, SpotLight)
nodes.rationale: No Brightness field — intensity = LightColor magnitude. LightColor = color * brightness. DirectionalLight has SkyColor, ShadowCascadeCount. PointLight has Radius (range). SpotLight has ConeInner/ConeOuter.
nodes.norm_label: verified light component api (directionallight, pointlight, spotlight)

nodes.label: Post-process components added to camera GameObject
nodes.rationale: Post-process effects (Bloom, Tonemapping, etc.) are added to the camera's GameObject. CameraComponent.EnablePostProcessing must be set to true. Bloom derives BasePostProcess<>.
nodes.norm_label: post-process components added to camera gameobject

nodes.label: apply_atmosphere and apply_post_fx_look preset tools
nodes.rationale: Compose existing primitive tools (add_light, set_fog, add_post_process) into mood presets. Extract primitive bodies into reusable static methods to share code paths between presets and primitives (DRY).

nodes.label: IBridgeHandler god node (176 edges)
nodes.rationale: IBridgeHandler is the most-connected abstraction in the graph (176 edges), serving as the spine that connects all bridge handlers across communities.

nodes.label: 1854 isolated nodes knowledge gaps
nodes.rationale: 1854 isolated nodes with <= 1 connection indicate possible missing edges or undocumented components in the knowledge graph.

nodes.label: Plugin version pinning (sbox-mcp-server@1.9.0)
nodes.rationale: The MCP server version is pinned in the plugin's .mcp.json so the addon/server pair can't silently drift. get_bridge_status warns if versions diverge.

nodes.label: Plugin bundled skills (sbox-build-feature, sbox-api, sbox-cookbook, sbox-scaffold-game, sbox-setup)
nodes.norm_label: plugin bundled skills (sbox-build-feature, sbox-api, sbox-cookbook, sbox-scaffold-game, sbox-setup)

nodes.label: Bridge addon not bundled — installed separately in project Libraries/
nodes.rationale: The plugin provides the MCP server side only. The bridge addon (C# editor code) must be installed separately into the s&box project's Libraries/ folder.
nodes.norm_label: bridge addon not bundled — installed separately in project libraries/

nodes.label: Health Component with IDamageable and IHealthEvents
nodes.rationale: [Sync, Change(nameof(OnHealthChanged))] fires on every client when owner changes health. IDamageable.OnDamage takes in DamageInfo as ref readonly struct. Only non-proxy mutates Current.
nodes.norm_label: health component with idamageable and ihealthevents

nodes.label: First-Person CharacterController (Example 2)
nodes.rationale: Uses lower-level CharacterController (no PlayerController helper). OnFixedUpdate for movement/physics; OnUpdate for camera/input. Movement in OnFixedUpdate to avoid jitter.

nodes.label: Hitscan Weapon with Rpc.Broadcast effects (Example 3)
nodes.rationale: Tracing runs only on shooter (IsProxy check). [Rpc.Broadcast] runs on ALL clients for effects. NetFlags.Unreliable for cosmetic effects. BreakFromPrefab() for standalone GameObjects.

nodes.label: GameObjectSystem GameManager with INetworkListener (Example 4)
nodes.rationale: GameObjectSystem<T> auto-instantiated per scene. ISceneStartup.OnHostInitialize runs once on host after scene load. INetworkListener.OnActive fires after connecting client fully loads scene.

nodes.label: Networked Player with Sync RPCs and Proxy Safety (Example 5)
nodes.rationale: [Sync] is owner→everyone by default. [Rpc.Owner] delivers to owning client. [Rpc.Broadcast] runs on all clients+host. [Rpc.Host] runs only on host. Scene.Directory.FindByGuid for passing GameObjects through RPCs.
nodes.norm_label: networked player with sync rpcs and proxy safety (example 5)

nodes.label: Razor HUD Panel with BuildHash data binding (Example 6)
nodes.rationale: BuildHash is the only way to make Razor tree rebuild cheaply. pointer-events:none on root lets game input pass through. :intro/:outro are s&box-specific transitions.
nodes.norm_label: razor hud panel with buildhash data binding (example 6)

nodes.label: Physics Grenade with ICollisionListener and async fuse (Example 7)
nodes.rationale: _ = FuseTimer() kicks off async countdown without awaiting. Component.Task scope cancels if GameObject destroyed. Scene.Trace.Sphere for radius damage. WithAnyTags for broad-phase filter.
nodes.norm_label: physics grenade with icollisionlistener and async fuse (example 7)

nodes.label: NavMeshAgent AI with State Machine (Example 8)
nodes.rationale: Perception runs at 4Hz (not per-frame). Agent.MoveTo is idempotent — cheap to call every tick with same target. Scene.NavMesh.GetRandomPoint returns Vector3? — null when no NavMesh.

nodes.label: PrefabSpawner with Pool-Friendly Lifecycle (Example 9)
nodes.rationale: Prefab.Clone creates instance with live link to prefab. NetworkSpawn() must only be called on authority. _alive.RemoveAll(go => !go.IsValid()) is idiomatic pruning of destroyed references.

nodes.label: Trigger Zone HealthPickup with ITriggerListener (Example 10)
nodes.rationale: Only host handles pickup. [Sync] on backing field so late-joining players see consumed pickups. Task.DelaySeconds auto-cancels when pickup is destroyed via implicit Component.Task scope.

nodes.label: s&box Anti-Patterns Quick Reference
nodes.rationale: Common mistakes: Unity API names (Update vs OnUpdate, Debug.Log vs Log.Info, Instantiate vs Clone), reading Input in OnFixedUpdate, mutating [Sync] fields on a proxy, building UI imperatively every frame.

nodes.label: Named input actions polled by name (not raw KeyboardCode)
nodes.rationale: Input is polled per-frame by NAME. Hardcoding KeyboardCode in gameplay breaks rebinding and gamepads. AnalogMove merges keyboard+stick; GetAnalog reads a single raw axis.
nodes.norm_label: named input actions polled by name (not raw keyboardcode)

nodes.label: IPressable interface with two real gotchas
nodes.rationale: Presses land on COLLIDER object not logical root — use PressablePropagate. IPressable.Release is unreliable — capture presser's Connection on Press and poll for release in OnUpdate.
nodes.norm_label: ipressable interface with two real gotchas

nodes.label: Analog feel from binary keys: rise/fall rate filter
nodes.rationale: Different rise vs fall rates plus deadzone snap-to-zero. Wind up slower than you let off. Gate raw input to zero when no driver. MathF may be unavailable in sandbox — confirm or hand-roll.
nodes.norm_label: analog feel from binary keys: rise/fall rate filter

nodes.label: ClientInput.PushScope for vehicle/turret/possession routing
nodes.rationale: using var scope = ClientInput.PushScope(player) wraps per-seat tick. Controllable components read seated player's input through the scope. Disposable scope restores prior scope on dispose.

nodes.label: Drag-to-aim in Razor via screen-space MousePanelEvent
nodes.rationale: Screen-space drag gestures expressed entirely in Razor panel with no InputAction. Camera.PointToScreenPixels converts world position to panel coordinates. Scene.Camera.Hud.DrawLine for immediate-mode lines.
nodes.norm_label: drag-to-aim in razor via screen-space mousepanelevent

nodes.label: UseInputControls + Mouse.Visibility to block input while UI open
nodes.rationale: Controller.UseInputControls = false freezes PlayerController movement/camera without nulling the component. Pair with Mouse.Visibility so cursor appears for clicking UI.
nodes.norm_label: useinputcontrols + mouse.visibility to block input while ui open

nodes.label: Three movement modes: Rigidbody-driven, Kinematic-integrated, CharacterController
nodes.rationale: Rigidbody-driven for props/debris. Kinematic-integrated (own velocity) when solver fights you (vehicles, dashes). CharacterController for swept-capsule but applies NO gravity — integrate gravity yourself.
nodes.norm_label: three movement modes: rigidbody-driven, kinematic-integrated, charactercontroller

nodes.label: Fluent Scene.Trace world query pattern
nodes.rationale: Build every query fluently, chain filters, then .Run(). Trace hits a child collider/hitbox not entity root — resolve owner with GetInAncestorsOrSelf<T>().

nodes.label: Going kinematic to own your velocity (Body.MotionEnabled = false)
nodes.rationale: Source 2 caps/damps velocity at contacts and silently eats applied drive force. Body.MotionEnabled = false takes movement off Source 2's hands. Body.Mass reads 0 when kinematic — keep your own mass field.
nodes.norm_label: going kinematic to own your velocity (body.motionenabled = false)

nodes.label: Multi-point spring-damper buoyancy (9-point hull sample)
nodes.rationale: 9 sample points over the hull each contributing spring (force proportional to depth) plus damper. Sampling at several offset points gives roll/pitch. Run in OnFixedUpdate gated IsProxy check.

nodes.label: Manual CCD via IScenePhysicsEvents.PrePhysicsStep
nodes.rationale: PrePhysicsStep runs after OnFixedUpdate but before the solver, so you can detect a tunnel and redirect the body before the engine sees the penetration. Running CCD inside OnFixedUpdate means body is already penetrating.

nodes.label: UI Razor PanelComponents HUDs & World UI Recipe (sbox-cookbook)
nodes.norm_label: ui razor panelcomponents huds & world ui recipe (sbox-cookbook)

nodes.label: BuildHash() contract — the single most important UI rule
nodes.rationale: Tree only rebuilds when BuildHash changes. List exactly the displayed state. Omit a field = UI never refreshes. Include per-frame value (Time.Now, raw position) = thrash full rebuild 60x/sec.
nodes.norm_label: buildhash() contract — the single most important ui rule

nodes.label: StateHasChanged() for imperative tree mutations
nodes.rationale: BuildHash only catches bound scalar field changes. When imperatively mutating the tree (append, prune) the framework won't notice — call StateHasChanged() after the mutation.

nodes.label: Bootstrap HUD at runtime via GameObjectSystem + ScreenPanel
nodes.rationale: Create HUD at runtime not in scene. Guard editor and dedicated-server contexts (Application.IsEditor && !Game.IsPlaying, Application.IsDedicatedServer) or you spam editor scene view and waste headless cycles.
nodes.norm_label: bootstrap hud at runtime via gameobjectsystem + screenpanel

nodes.label: World-space panel on prop screen face via Sandbox.WorldPanel
nodes.rationale: Sizing is NOT pixels — convert through WorldPanel.ScreenToWorldScale. Keep per-model offset table for local Position/Rotation/Size. Lazily retry init until panel is valid.
nodes.norm_label: world-space panel on prop screen face via sandbox.worldpanel

nodes.label: Async UI guard with revision counter + CancellationToken
nodes.rationale: Gate every async continuation behind a revision counter + CancellationToken. Bump revision and cancel CTS on every new op so in-flight tasks become no-ops. Old animations clobber new state otherwise.
nodes.norm_label: async ui guard with revision counter + cancellationtoken

nodes.label: WorldPanel batching collision avoidance (unique micro-scale offset)
nodes.rationale: UI batcher merges WorldPanels at the same render scale causing visual desync. Fix: unique micro-offset (baseScale + bucketIndex * 0.0001f) bucketed by stable hash. StableWorldPanelAnchor re-snaps to absolute world-space every OnUpdate and OnPreRender.
nodes.norm_label: worldpanel batching collision avoidance (unique micro-scale offset)

nodes.label: Casino trust posture: external-backend vs host-authoritative
nodes.rationale: External-backend (sino.s_sino): s&box is thin presentation client, all money math on external Node/WebSocket server. Host-authoritative (lavagame.multis_cases): mixed trust, host re-validates every staked action. Pick up front as it dictates the netcode.
nodes.norm_label: casino trust posture: external-backend vs host-authoritative

nodes.label: Server-side roll decoupled from reveal animation
nodes.rationale: The roll must be server-side and the reveal must be local cosmetic that cannot change the odds. Decide the winner first, animate second. The strip is a cosmetic lie.
nodes.norm_label: server-side roll decoupled from reveal animation

nodes.label: EV-preserving house-edge normalization formula
nodes.rationale: Scale all item values so weighted EV lands at Price * targetRatio (~0.75 = 25% house edge). Redistribute anything clipped by per-item value cap back onto uncapped items so rarity ratios survive. THE reusable 'set the payout %' routine.

nodes.label: Provably-fair commit/reveal scheme for casino rolls
nodes.rationale: Server picks serverSeed, shows SHA256(serverSeed) before bet. Client supplies clientSeed+nonce. Roll = HMAC_SHA256(serverSeed, clientSeed:nonce). s&box sandbox has no System.Security.Cryptography — do HMAC on a backend or use FNV-1a/XOR in-sandbox (integrity, not cryptographic fairness).
nodes.norm_label: provably-fair commit/reveal scheme for casino rolls

nodes.label: Currency as cents-as-strings (never float for bankrolls)
nodes.rationale: Server holds the ledger; client is a corrected cache. Cents-as-strings end-to-end so 12-digit jackpot never touches long/float. CompareCentsStrings compares by length-then-ordinal.
nodes.norm_label: currency as cents-as-strings (never float for bankrolls)

nodes.label: Steam-host-id server whitelist anti-piracy (ServerVerifier.cs)
nodes.rationale: Un-spoofable Connection.Host.SteamId checked against cloud whitelist. If unlisted, disable saving and kick (GameManager.BlockSaving = true then Game.Disconnect()).

nodes.label: IUse interaction interface (the kitchen interaction spine)
nodes.rationale: IUse with CanUse/OnUse/LookingAt. Player runs one per-frame trace, tracks focused IUse, fires LookingAt on focus changes. Every station implements IUse. To add a station: implement the interface, no dispatcher change.
nodes.norm_label: iuse interaction interface (the kitchen interaction spine)

nodes.label: Carryable items with [Sync] PlayerHolder and physics-off-while-held
nodes.rationale: Carried item disables its own physics while held and re-enables when dropped. Hand-off falls out for free: player A drops → item becomes physical on counter → player B looks at it and picks up. No bespoke transfer RPC needed.
nodes.norm_label: carryable items with [sync] playerholder and physics-off-while-held

nodes.label: State-on-the-object assembly (no Recipe asset)
nodes.rationale: The 'recipe' is the accumulated component state on the item built across stations and graded at delivery. Stack List<BoxType> Type and carry state forward. Enables recombinant assembly (any order, combos).

nodes.label: Order board with interval spawn, soft cap, per-order timers
nodes.rationale: Continuous procedural spawner with soft cap. Designer-authored item/label pools. Per-order MaxTime/TimeRemaining — harder combos get more time. Host ticks each ticket.
nodes.norm_label: order board with interval spawn, soft cap, per-order timers

nodes.label: Co-op kitchen networking anti-patterns (score/validation host-only)
nodes.rationale: wjse ships real footguns: score and validation run wherever the delivering client is with no Networking.IsHost re-check. Correct: validate+score on host, [Sync] the result. Don't broadcast order list every frame.

nodes.label: Per-source Set/Add/Mult stat modifier engine
nodes.rationale: Dictionary<IStatModifier, Dictionary<PlayerStat, ModifierData>> keyed by source for clean removal. RefreshProperty: highest-priority Set, then sum Adds, then multiply Mults, then clamp. THE defining system — build it first.

nodes.label: Reflection-driven perk draft catalog with weighted draw
nodes.rationale: TypeLibrary.GetTypes<Perk>() builds the pool. Filter by attribute axes (rarity/level/prereq/difficulty/curse). Weighted draw-without-replacement. Adding a perk = one file. Same code drives level-up, boss reward, and curse draft.
nodes.norm_label: reflection-driven perk draft catalog with weighted draw

nodes.label: Perk event-bus via IPlayerCallbacks virtual hooks + fan-out
nodes.rationale: Perk base implements IPlayerCallbacks with ~45 virtual hooks. Player fans every game event to all owned perks+loadout items. Adding a reactive upgrade = override one method, no central dispatch wiring. Pure open/closed extension.
nodes.norm_label: perk event-bus via iplayercallbacks virtual hooks + fan-out

nodes.label: Uniform-grid spatial hash broadphase for 350+ enemies
nodes.rationale: Bucket arena into uniform grid. Thing re-registers cell only when crossing a boundary. Test only 3x3 neighborhood filtered by CollideWith type list before circle-overlap. Powers boids-like enemy separation.
nodes.norm_label: uniform-grid spatial hash broadphase for 350+ enemies

nodes.label: Perks synced as identity→level, never as objects
nodes.rationale: Host keeps real Dictionary<int, Perk>. Proxies get [Sync] NetDictionary<int,int> (typeIdentity→level). Host pre-renders name/description/icon into parallel [Sync] NetList<string> so clients never run perk logic.
nodes.norm_label: perks synced as identity→level, never as objects

nodes.label: Four inventory shapes: Counts, Slots/Hotbar, Grid, Per-instance
nodes.rationale: Counts (Dictionary<string,int>), Slots/hotbar (List<InventorySlot>), Grid/spatial (bool[W,H] occupancy), Per-instance items (wrapper class with mutable state). Pick shape up front — they don't mix cleanly.
nodes.norm_label: four inventory shapes: counts, slots/hotbar, grid, per-instance

nodes.label: Host-authoritative multiplayer pickup (BasePickup with double-pickup prevention)
nodes.rationale: Spawned world items must be removed exactly once, by the host. Guard on Networking.IsHost in both ITriggerListener and ICollisionListener paths to prevent double pickup.
nodes.norm_label: host-authoritative multiplayer pickup (basepickup with double-pickup prevention)

nodes.label: Race-locked pickup via tag-as-mutex (tag 'picked_up')
nodes.rationale: If several clients fire a pickup RPC in the same tick, obj.IsValid() is still true for all. Tag the object as a mutex before end-of-frame Destroy(), then broadcast the confirmed result.

nodes.label: Inventory reconnect snapshot via GetRawNetworkData
nodes.rationale: On reconnect host takes ownership of sleeping body, clones fresh player, pushes saved state via targeted RPCs. GetRawNetworkData extracts Dictionary<int,InventoryItem> and Dictionary<string,int>. Then RpcOwnerRestoredInventory restores exactly what was there.

nodes.label: Leaderboard is a query over a stat, not a separate object
nodes.rationale: A leaderboard is not a separate object you write to — it is a query over a stat. Write with Stats.SetValue/Increment; read ranked table back with Leaderboards.GetFromStat(statName). The stat name string is the contract.
nodes.norm_label: leaderboard is a query over a stat, not a separate object

nodes.label: Host cannot write another player's stat — must round-trip to owning client
nodes.rationale: Sandbox.Services.Stats always writes to the CALLING client's own Steam account. In multiplayer must round-trip write to owning client via [Rpc.Broadcast] and check Connection.Local.SteamId == steamId before writing.
nodes.norm_label: host cannot write another player's stat — must round-trip to owning client

nodes.label: Stats.Flush() is platform-rate-limited — throttle to 12-120s
nodes.rationale: Don't flush per-frame. Throttle (vault77 = 12s, enifun = 60s, lavagame = 120s) and only push on changed values.

nodes.label: Two-authority stat callback: owner writes cloud, host writes synced truth
nodes.rationale: In a single OnTriggerEnter: local owner (!IsProxy) writes to Stats (only owner can write its SteamId); host (Networking.IsHost) mutates the authoritative synced Scorecard. Don't conflate who owns the cloud stat with who owns gameplay truth.
nodes.norm_label: two-authority stat callback: owner writes cloud, host writes synced truth

nodes.label: Host-authoritative [Sync(SyncFlags.FromHost)] economy state
nodes.rationale: Put currency + every upgrade level on one component as [Sync(SyncFlags.FromHost)]. SyncFlags.FromHost means clients literally cannot write the value. Collections cannot [Sync] directly — use NetList/NetDictionary or CSV-string [Sync].

nodes.label: Canonical buy recipe: proxy→host→validate→spend→cap→save
nodes.rationale: The most-copied block across the corpus. Re-check the cap and funds ON THE HOST, never trust the client number. With per-owner permissions, gate on the Rpc.Caller.

nodes.label: Cost curve: geometric (BaseCost * pow(mult, level)) vs table (explicit array)
nodes.rationale: Geometric: BaseCost * pow(mult, currentLevel), returns sentinel at max. Table: static readonly float[] of explicit per-tier costs. XP-to-level usually a pure power curve solved both ways, looping to handle multiple level-ups.
nodes.norm_label: cost curve: geometric (basecost * pow(mult, level)) vs table (explicit array)

nodes.label: Pull-based payoff: consuming systems query, upgrades don't push
nodes.rationale: Upgrades should NOT reach into gameplay components. Consuming systems query the current effect. Central EffectsManager cleanest — upgrades declare UpgradeEffect structs and never touch gameplay.
nodes.norm_label: pull-based payoff: consuming systems query, upgrades don't push

nodes.label: GameResource upgrades for data-driven content authoring
nodes.rationale: [AssetType] decorated GameResource for upgrades. ResourceLibrary.GetAll<UpgradeResource>() to enumerate. Never send a GameResource ref over an RPC — send Id string and reconstruct host-side. Never cache an empty GetAll() — hotload race may produce it.
nodes.norm_label: gameresource upgrades for data-driven content authoring

nodes.label: Switch-expression effect lookups (no stored numbers, no migration)
nodes.rationale: Store only the int level; resolve every effect through a switch expression. Balance change never needs a save migration. Buy path transactional with rollback: deduct, run applyEffect() in try, refund on exception, then commit with non-throttled save.
nodes.norm_label: switch-expression effect lookups (no stored numbers, no migration)

nodes.label: State enum with [Sync(SyncFlags.FromHost)] setter as transition hook
nodes.rationale: FromHost means clients cannot write it — only host mutates. Putting side-effect switch in the setter (not a [Change] callback) means host runs entry logic exactly once on transition. Guard re-entry with if(_state == next) return.
nodes.norm_label: state enum with [sync(syncflags.fromhost)] setter as transition hook

nodes.label: Editor-placed manager must NetworkSpawn itself for [Sync] to replicate
nodes.rationale: The #1 bite: [Sync] does nothing until the host NetworkSpawns the manager GameObject. An object dropped in the scene is not networked by default.
nodes.norm_label: editor-placed manager must networkspawn itself for [sync] to replicate

nodes.label: Host migration resets host-only state — OnBecameHost must hard-reset
nodes.rationale: Promoted client has all private arrays null and stale [Sync] State. On OnBecameHost, hard-reset/rebuild scene-scanned objects before trusting anything. Keep _hostInitialized fallback in OnUpdate. Set LobbyConfig.AutoSwitchToBestHost=false to prevent migration of a live match.
nodes.norm_label: host migration resets host-only state — onbecamehost must hard-reset

nodes.label: State-as-component alternative to enum-switch (despawn.murder pattern)
nodes.rationale: Each phase is its own RoundState : Component subclass with Begin/Tick/Finish lifecycle. Manager holds active state component and transitions by adding/removing it. Copy per-phase data OUT before Finish() destroys old state's components.

nodes.label: Tag-based player membership (no manager-side List<Player>)
nodes.rationale: Store per-player round membership as networked tags on each player GameObject, not in a manager-side List<Player>. Fresh mid-round joiner auto-detected in OnUpdate with zero list-reconciliation — single biggest correctness win for drop-in co-op.

nodes.label: File-Based IPC Architecture
nodes.rationale: s&box sandboxed C# blocks System.Net (no HttpListener, WebSocket, TCP), so file-based IPC through %TEMP%/sbox-bridge-ipc/ is used instead. MCP server writes request JSON; addon polls on the main editor thread and writes responses back.

nodes.label: Screenshot-Driven Build Workflow
nodes.rationale: After visual changes, use screenshot_from to aim at the target then read the PNG. Claude is multimodal — guessing about visual outcomes from code produces long iteration loops. take_screenshot only renders the Main Camera's fixed angle.

nodes.label: IPC Directory Mismatch Gotcha
nodes.rationale: Node reads TEMP env var while C# reads TMP env var, causing the MCP server and addon to watch different temp dirs. Set SBOX_BRIDGE_IPC_DIR to the same absolute path on both sides.

nodes.label: Libraries/ Folder Requirement
nodes.rationale: The addon must live in the project's Libraries/ folder, not s&box's global addons/ folder. The global folder is built-in-only and silently refuses to compile custom C#.

nodes.label: Gotcha #1: Bridge Cannot Synthesize Gameplay Input
nodes.rationale: simulate_input calls SetAction once, inside a single editor frame. The rising edge (Input.Pressed) frequently misses because press+release collapse into one frame. Analog injection (AnalogMove/AnalogLook) has no setter at all. drive_player is a partial workaround but cannot replace human playtesting.
nodes.norm_label: gotcha #1: bridge cannot synthesize gameplay input

nodes.label: Gotcha #2: Default Surface Not Found on Scene.Trace
nodes.rationale: After a long or messy session, every Scene.Trace throws 'Default Surface not found'. The editor surface/physics asset registry gets into a bad state. Fix: restart_editor. Auto-detected in v1.10.0 with recoverable:true hint.
nodes.norm_label: gotcha #2: default surface not found on scene.trace

nodes.label: Gotcha #3: New PackageReference Needs Real Restart
nodes.rationale: trigger_hotload recompiles against the already-resolved reference graph. A newly-added local-library PackageReference changes that graph itself; only a full editor launch re-resolves it. Proactively warned in v1.10.0.
nodes.norm_label: gotcha #3: new packagereference needs real restart

nodes.label: Gotcha #4: Community Model Paths Render as ERROR Mesh
nodes.rationale: s&box resolves model paths against your project's compiled assets. Community models need their full dependency chain (.vmdl + .vmat + .vtex) copied into Assets/. Engine built-ins (models/dev/*, materials/default/*) always resolve globally.
nodes.norm_label: gotcha #4: community model paths render as error mesh

nodes.label: Gotcha #5: Shadowing Core Asset Causes Endless Recompile Loop
nodes.rationale: Copying a model to a path that collides with a core engine asset (models/citizen/**) causes s&box to enter an infinite recompile/asset-processing loop. Fix: namespace community models under project-namespaced paths, never core tree paths.
nodes.norm_label: gotcha #5: shadowing core asset causes endless recompile loop

nodes.label: Gotcha #6: Razor/UI Transpiler Quirks
nodes.rationale: PanelComponent renders nothing without a sibling ScreenPanel. Emoji or switch-expressions in @code blocks can crash the Razor transpiler. Root type-selector SCSS rules are silently skipped (use class selectors). 'Error opening stylesheet .razor.scss (File not found)' is a harmless probe.

nodes.label: Gotcha #7: Whitelist-Blocked APIs Masked by Broken-Reference Cascade
nodes.rationale: s&box runs sandboxed game code against an API whitelist. When game code fails, a broken-reference cascade masks the real whitelist rejection. Use read_log with filter 'Error |' to dig out the real error. Known: array.Clone() -> array.ToArray(), System.Math/MathF -> MathX.
nodes.norm_label: gotcha #7: whitelist-blocked apis masked by broken-reference cascade

nodes.label: Gotcha #8: take_screenshot 30s Timeout = GPU/Render Stall
nodes.rationale: The capture path needs the renderer to produce a frame. If the GPU/render pipeline has stalled (ToolsStallMonitor fires), the frame never completes. Fix: restart_editor. Saved scene survives the restart.

nodes.label: The Brain: sbox-cookbook as AI Knowledge Base
nodes.rationale: The hardest part of building an s&box game with AI isn't writing C# — it's knowing the right pattern. sbox-cookbook provides code-grounded recipes mined from real shipped open-source s&box games so the AI reaches for proven patterns instead of guessing.
nodes.norm_label: the brain: sbox-cookbook as ai knowledge base

nodes.label: AI Hallucination Problem with Fast-Moving APIs
